raw_response:
I agree with the final selection of Option C. By specifically targeting the inclination of the cusps—ensuring the upper cusp moves toward the cheekside and the lower cusp moves tongue-side—we effectively break down any locking mechanism caused by full-edge contact. This targeted modification maintains optimal occlusal relationships crucial for preventing wear and discomfort associated with continuous edge-to-edge contact. Thus, my agreement stands firm with no additional reasons needed beyond those already provided. My chosen answer remains **Option C).**
